arguments.query: MongoDB Atlas MCP server AI agent toolkit
MongoDB Atlas API key self-serve free tier developer access
MongoDB official MCP server GitHub repository mongodb mcp server
MongoDB Atlas free tier sign up API access self-serve no credit card
MongoDB Atlas Administration API endpoints list clusters projects users monitoring

researched_category.notes: MongoDB Atlas is a multi-cloud developer data platform providing a fully managed document database service.

one_liner.value: Multi-cloud document database service with built-in administration API, search, vector search, and data federation capabilities.

methods.notes: Primary: OAuth 2.0 (Service Accounts with Client Credentials flow). Legacy: API keys with HTTP Digest Authentication. For actual database data access, standard MongoDB credential authentication (username/password) via connection string is used.

access.notes: Anyone can sign up for a free MongoDB Atlas account (M0 cluster, no credit card required). Organization Owners can create API keys or service accounts programmatically. No paid plan required for basic API access, though some API operations (e.g., provisioning dedicated clusters) may incur charges.

summary.value: The Atlas Administration API is a comprehensive REST API covering projects, clusters, database users, network access, monitoring, alerts, backups, performance advisor, and more. It is fully documented with OpenAPI spec. Additionally, the MongoDB wire protocol (drivers) is used for data CRUD operations. The official MongoDB MCP Server provides agent-friendly access to both Administration and data operations.

agent_interface.notes: MongoDB provides an official MCP Server (mongodb-js/mongodb-mcp-server) that enables AI agents to interact with MongoDB deployments. It supports both database operations (CRUD, aggregations) and Atlas administration (cluster management, users). Bundled plugins exist for Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, and Gemini. The Atlas CLI also provides full API coverage.

rationale.value: MongoDB Atlas has a fully documented, broad REST API (Atlas Administration API) with self-serve free tier access and OAuth 2.0 authentication. An official MCP Server exists and is actively maintained by MongoDB. Developers can sign up for free, generate API credentials immediately, and integrate with AI agents via the MCP server or direct REST API calls. There are no material blockers for building an agent toolkit today.

s1.quote: The Atlas Administration API follows the principles of the REST architectural style to expose a number of internal resources that enable programmatic access to administrative features in Atlas.

s2.quote: Service accounts are the recommended method to manage authentication to the Atlas Administration API. Service accounts provide improved security over API keys by using the industry standard OAuth 2.0 protocol with the Client Credentials flow.

s3.quote: To use the Atlas Administration API to manage your Atlas clusters, you must authenticate your API requests with one of the following authentication methods: Service account access tokens (OAuth 2.0), API keys (HTTP Digest Authentication).

s4.quote: Yes, MongoDB Atlas offers a free-forever tier, the M0. Atlas's free tier includes 512 MB of storage... Get started with a free tier

s5.quote: The Atlas Administration API follows the principles of the REST architectural style to expose a number of internal resources that enable programmatic access to administrative features in Atlas.

s6.quote: A Model Context Protocol server for interacting with MongoDB Databases and MongoDB Atlas.
s6.title: mongodb-js/mongodb-mcp-server

s7.quote: To create a service account or API keys for an organization, you must have Organization Owner access to that organization.

s8.quote: Atlas App Services has reached its end-of-life status and is no longer actively supported by MongoDB.

s9.quote: The MongoDB MCP Server is our official MCP server implementation that gives your AI client a set of tools that can be used to interact with your MongoDB data and deployments.

s10.quote: MongoDB Atlas - The multi-cloud developer data platform available on AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud

s11.quote: If you use Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, or Gemini, you can install the MongoDB MCP Server as part of the official MongoDB plugin or extension for your AI client.

s12.quote: Request to add MongoDB as an official Composio toolkit, enabling AI agents to interact with any MongoDB deployment (Atlas, self-hosted, or local)
s12.title: [Tool Request]: MongoDB #2984 - ComposioHQ/composio

limitations: The Data API (HTTPS-based CRUD) is deprecated/end-of-life; data operations now require MongoDB drivers or the MCP server.
Some advanced Atlas API operations (e.g., provisioning dedicated clusters) will incur billing charges even if API access itself is free.
The MCP server is in public preview (as per the MongoDB blog announcement), though it is production-ready with 70+ releases.
